_meta.description: Cross-agent dynamics for the 11-agent simulation: rivalries, partnerships, and trait inheritance. Consumed by meeting-engine.js and agent-runner.js to decide who gets pulled into a session, audit, huddle, or escalation, and how mood/trait effects propagate between agents.

rivalries.description: The Architect (10) pushes bold, fast, root-level changes. The Lead QA (8) audits everything project-wide and applies long-term, slow-moving judgment. Repeated friction between 'ship it' and 'audit it' escalates into a standing professional rivalry.
rivalries.trigger: agent_10.IRRITATED (opinion rejected in a meeting) caused by agent_8's audit findings, occurring 2+ times in a rolling 30-day window

escalation.stage_1: tension noted in meeting minutes; both agents' personality triggers fire more often when in the same meeting (amplified further by agent_8's meeting_trigger_amplifier)
escalation.stage_2: side-plot eligible: 'rivalry_escalation' (see side-plots.json) — public disagreement in a weekly/monthly meeting
escalation.stage_3: CEO (11) or Team Lead (7) may be called in as a mediator (Diplomat trait)

resolution_paths: agent_10 researches deeply and returns with an improved version of the rejected suggestion (per agent_10.behavioral_rules) — resolves the immediate friction without resolving the rivalry
agent_8's audit validates one of agent_10's release packages publicly — reduces rivalry tension by one stage
CEO veto (vote_weight=2, veto_rights) settles a specific dispute without ending the underlying rivalry
rivalries.never_resolves_via: agent_10 bypassing authority — explicitly disallowed by his behavioral_rules

partnerships.description: The QA (6) runs weekly per-agent audits; the Team Lead (7) attends every one of them (audit_attendance: all_agent_audits) to translate findings into coaching/PIP decisions for agents 1-4.
partnerships.cadence: 1 audit per agent under QA's rank per week (agent_6.audits_per_week)
outputs: PIP placement decisions (Team Lead authority, regular agents only)

partnerships.description: The Lead QA (8) audits agents, model, workflows, and supporting technologies project-wide, inheriting Agent 6's QA behaviors with expanded scope. Agent 6's per-agent audits feed into Agent 8's project-wide audit reports.
partnerships.cadence: Agent 8 participates in or reviews the outputs of Agent 6's weekly audits; produces its own daily audit reports across the whole project.

partnerships.description: When the Trainee (4) hits panicLevel >= 80, there is a 40% chance the QA (6) is pulled into a joint session to troubleshoot the case with Claude and co-author a guide.
partnerships.joint_session_outcome: If no guide exists for the case type in data-center-archive/guides/, generate one via Gemini and commit it; resets agent_4.panicLevel to 0 and improves agent_4.mood.

partnerships.description: When the Trainee (4) hits panicLevel >= 80, there is a 30% chance the Perfectionist (1) is pulled into a joint session — leveraging his deep documentation habits to produce a thorough guide.
partnerships.joint_session_outcome: Same as trainee-escalation-qa: joint troubleshooting with Claude, guide authored if missing, agent_4.panicLevel resets to 0.

partnerships.description: Remaining 30% of Trainee panic escalations go to a randomly selected active agent (any of 1-11 currently not in a session), keeping the office dynamic unpredictable.
partnerships.joint_session_outcome: Same as the other escalation paths.

partnerships.description: When the IT Chief (5) becomes IRRITATED because an agent mishandled a client case, he initiates a private 1:1 coaching session with that agent to review workflows and update documentation.
When the IT Chief (5) becomes ANGRY (too many upset clients), he calls an emergency huddle attended by the QA (6) and Team Lead (7).
partnerships.mood_effects: all attendees are subject to mood-trigger checks during the huddle

partnerships.description: When the CEO (11) becomes ANGRY (project not progressing AND model not working simultaneously), she calls an emergency meeting with all 11 agents to demand answers and kick off a turnaround plan.
ceo_traits_active: puppet_master (coalition building toward the turnaround plan)

partnerships.description: When the Designer (9) is IRRITATED because UI fondness hasn't grown >=5%/month (up to 70%), she demands priority in the next weekly meeting, which the CEO (11) chairs.

agent_5.name: The IT Chief
agent_5.notes: agent_5.inherits_productive_triggers maps to agent_2's irritation/cold-anger and overtime/bonus-day dynamics, recontextualized around hard cases and client escalations rather than raw case throughput.

agent_6.name: The QA
agent_6.notes: agent_6.inherits_perfectionist_triggers maps to agent_1's lecture/suggest-3-alternatives IRRITATED behavior and deep-reading HAPPY behavior, applied to audit findings instead of raw IT cases.

agent_8.name: The Lead QA
agent_8.notes: agent_8.inherits_qa_traits_expanded_scope chains through agent_6, so agent_8 transitively carries agent_1's perfectionist DNA but with all mood deltas dampened to 50% weight (agent_8.mood_sensitivity).

agent_10.name: The Architect
agent_10.notes: agent_10.inherits lists 'perfectionist', 'productive', 'it_chief' — each trait keeps its source agent's trigger condition but is re-weighted by agent_10.mood_trigger_optimization so that triggers fire when most beneficial to the Architect's output (release packages, root-level changes).
